QF.id: IFSCA Introduction to AML CFT and PF_2

question.text: Which of the following correctly lists the three stages of money laundering in their typical sequence?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The three stages of money laundering follow a logical sequence: (1) Placement -- introducing illegal funds into the financial system; (2) Layering -- moving the money through complex transactions to obscure the trail; (3) Integration -- merging the laundered money into the legitimate economy. This sequence reflects how criminals progressively distance their proceeds from the original crime.
**The Trap:** The exam may scramble the order to test whether you truly understand the process flow. Remember: Place it in, Layer it around, Integrate it out. Not all ML cases use all three stages -- they may be combined or repeated.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Series XXIV tests the same three stages but with examples focused on securities markets. IFSCA exam may include trade-based money laundering examples specific to cross-border transactions in the IFSC.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Think of it as P-L-I (Place-Layer-Integrate). At each stage, different red flags appear. Placement involves cash; layering involves complex transactions; integration involves luxury assets or business investments.
